What I would like to do now is teach a chunk that you can place this anywhere you want to. But what I want to share with you about having a speech or having a training is this. If you will teach people something about finances, you can get more money for your training. So somehow or another try to think, how could I teach a wealth formula? How could I teach good information that might be helpful to someone? So the first chunk that I want to share with you has to do with basic financial information. And as you teach it, your audience will leave there with the idea, you know, this works. I will be wealthy. So one of the things I want you to understand is this. There are two concepts or two methods of making money. And on your paper I want you to write linear income, and on the other side I want you to write residual income. Linear income is when you work for the money. Residual income is where your money works for the money. Now obviously which type of income would you rather have? Residual income. But now don't make a decisive decision. I merely want us to understand that there are two types of income. We would prefer residual income, but I'm also going to tell you that if you have inside you what it takes to be a winner, to be successful, like my mother always asked me, Johnny. When are you going to make enough money? I mean like when's enough enough? And I said, Mom, the same thing that's in me that made me successful is that same thing that will never let me stop. I can't stop going to the next level, achieving things, setting objectives and achieving those objectives. So I've got news for you guys. You'll never stop learning. You'll never stop achieving because what's going to give you that burning desire is going to keep you from doing it. And until my dying day I'll probably be out here teaching people how to be a more effective presenter of information because I love it. I live it and I breathe it. And if you ever have a question by the way, you can call me because you're not imposing on my time. You're talking about what I love and what better conversation to have. There's only one thing that I know of that's better to talk about than how to give a good presentation and that's talking about me. I can talk about both of those subjects endlessly. But there are two types of income, linear income, residual income. Linear income, there are two types. One is called time for money, the other is called results for money. You really need to understand the difference between these two. Time for money means you have a job. You are exchanging a certain number of your hours of time for a paycheck, let's just say 40 hours. When you get your first job, it might be at McDonald's, Burger King, whatever your first job was, Moen Longs was mine. It was very simple. I gave them 40 hours, they gave me a paycheck. By the way, was your paycheck anywhere near what your paycheck is today? Probably not. Why? Well, inflation was one thing, but basically the jobs that you get in the beginning are jobs that the reason they don't pay you much is all they need is a body that can say, would you like fries with that? We fill a time slot. Why is it, see people spend their entire life trying to get more money for the same thing. You want to give 40 hours, but I want to get a bigger paycheck. Why would someone give you a bigger paycheck if you're going to give them the same thing, which is 40 hours? Can you get any answers on that? Because you have more experience, you have more communication skills, you know how to do more things. Now how many of you would like to know how to double your income next week? Okay, here's the answer. Go out and get a second job doing the same thing you do on your first job. Now wouldn't that work? Yeah, that'll work. If you want to make more money, give them more time. Oh no, no, no, no, no, I don't want to do that. You see what happens, we understand a certain thing, but I know a lot of people that have two and three jobs and it's not to become wealthy. It's just to break even. Okay, but you learn what works. But we also learned that you don't have to give them extra time if you could give them more expertise. A brain surgeon gives a certain amount of time and gives a big paycheck. So we get education, we develop our skills, and we're constantly trying to learn more and more so that we get paid more money for the same time. All of that comes into the category called time for money. There's another method of earning money. It's called results for money. These are where commission salespeople are. These are where entrepreneurs are. Business owners. Real estate investors go out here and say, okay, I'm going to buy a house, fix it up, and sell it, and make a big profit. Results for money. Here's the good and here's the bad. The good is if you are in the results for money economy, you normally will make way more money than in the time for money economy. But there are no guarantees. None. Somebody say, you want to go on a straight commission? I say, yeah. If I never had any experience, I say, I don't think so. Because I'm not going to go out here and not knowing what I'm doing and be on strictly results for money. So can you grow into these areas? Most of by show of hands, how many of you recognize results for money would probably pay more money? So you need to understand that. But are there any guarantees? No. The guarantee is if you want a job, no guarantees on results for money. If you are a public speaker and you are paid based on sales that would be made at your event. So is it time for money or results for money? Results for money. If you don't sell and there are no results, they're not going to pay you. Attorneys, attorneys have historically had, they charge a lot per hour. My son's an attorney. His wife is a CPA. I told her the bloom joke. She didn't think it was funny. My son, the attorney, I told him the joke. Do you know the difference between a dead skunk on the highway and a dead attorney on the highway? Sometimes they're skid marks in front of the skunk. He didn't think that was funny. Some people just don't have a good sense of humor, right? Okay, but attorneys have learned that they're shifting from the time for money to the results for money. Tell you what, you don't pay me my $200 an hour. I will represent you in your lawsuit. I'll handle all expenses and then I'll get a percentage of what I collect for you. And if I collect money for you, you pay me out of that. If I don't collect money for you, you owe me nothing. Now, how many of you, knowing it was going to cost you more money, would probably pay more money if it was based on results? Could I say a show of hands on that? That's why I turned. So, when you look at results for money, it has an upside, a tremendous upside. People normally make more money. I have the most expensive training in America, but it's based on results. If it doesn't work for you, they don't have to pay me. And I found more people opt for that than a fixed price that would be a lower one. It's called time for money, results for money. Now the other is called residual income. Residual income is when you own things that produce income. What if you had a whole bunch of vending machines out here? You own them. Every time somebody comes up there, sticks a dollar in there, they get a Coca-Cola, you make money. You own assets. I own a lot of real estate. I own an asset and that asset produces money. Okay, so what you're looking for are understanding that there are three ways of making money. Any questions on that? Okay, so with an understanding, you can now take the second step in a financial process and that is, let's learn the wealth formula. The wealth formula has four parts and I will share with you right now. If you learn the wealth formula and implement the formula, you will be wealthy. And I'm going to convince you of that by sharing this formula with you. There are four parts of the formula. Number one, if you want to get anything out, you have to put something in. Would you agree with that? Okay, so you will have to make a determination as to how much of your money you're going to put into an investment account. Now let me define investment account. This is a checking account, a savings account, a brokerage account, some type of financial account that you literally put your money into and then you manage that. That's what I mean by an investment account. If you go down to banks and say, I need one of those investment accounts, they're not going to have it. We've got a checking account, we've got savings account, we don't know what an investment account is. Okay, that's my definition. What are you going to put in? Now since I told you there are four parts of the formula, for teaching purposes only, I am going to answer three of these questions for you. I'm going to pick a number for you. How much are you going to put in? You're going to put in $165 a month. I show off hands, how many of you could afford that and not penalize your family one iota? Okay, now let me throw in one of them. It will, you will become wealthy if you put that money in and leave it alone. Point number two in the wealth formula. How long will you let this program work for you and not pull money out of it? Because if you start pulling money out of it, it's going to kill you. That's the reason I keep them out small that you put in there. I'm going to pick a number for you here. The number is 25 years. If you'll put $165 a month in this account for 25 years, you will be wealthy. But there's two more parts of the formula, right? The third part of the formula to me is the most crucial. Now what's the purpose of putting $165 into an investment account? What do you think you're going to be doing with this? Investing it. Third part of the formula is this. What yield will you learn how to get? What yield will you learn how to get? Now once again, what's the formula for determining yield? Take the profit, divide it by how much you had invested and it will tell you what percent your yield was. Now let me give you some examples of that. You can invest your money at 0% or you can invest your money at 3% or 6%, which would you prefer? 6, simple math, isn't it? The higher the number you choose, but wait a minute, what if you want to get up to 10? Pretty soon, but I don't know how to invest at 10. Ah, that requires what? Knowledge. So that's the reason I say what yield will you learn how to get? Fourth part of the formula is this. How big do you want this investment account to grow for your retirement? What's the magic number? How much money do you want for your retirement plan? And there's just four parts of the formula. What are you going to put in? How long are you going to leave it there? What yield will you get on your money? And how much are you going to have when you get through? It's a math formula, isn't it? Okay, the answer to number four is $1 million. $1 million. The variable is number three. You're going to put in $165 a month for how many years? 25. How much money are you going to take out each month? None. What about the profits? None. Leave it in there. Let them grow. It's called compounding. All right. In your notes, I want you to have about four inches in your notes to write this chart down. You will need probably four, five, six lines on here. I want you to put column one. Column one will be the yield on your money. So the first line, I want you to put zero percent. How many of you know how to invest money at zero percent? Okay, where would you do that? Three plate. Mattress is the number one answer. Buried in a fruit jar. Give it to your spouse. Okay, put it somewhere that pays no yield whatsoever. Okay, column two, put 25 years. On three, what is the end result? You would have put $49,000. You can put 49K if you want to be abbreviated here. $49,000. Meaning if you put $165 a month under your mattress for 25 years, you'd have a little over $49,000 at the end of 25 years. Now you're thinking, where is he going with this? I want to do a test. How many of you know someone who's been an adult for 25 years that does not have $49,000? Oh, I didn't ask if it was you. I said you know somebody. That's like, I have a friend who? Okay, now one of two things either occurred. They either never learned the wealth formula or they did not implement the wealth formula. Okay? Raise the bar a little bit. Drop down one line and put 3%. If you had put $165 a month in for 25 years but you invested that money at 3%, you would have $73,000. 73K. Now wait a minute. I don't want to get above your head. Where do you know you can invest money at 3%? Go to a bank and ask them the difference between a checking account and a what? Savings account. Normally they'll say something like, well, on checking we don't pay any interest but on savings we'll pay you something like 3% a year. Then you would have $73,000 at the end of 25 years. How many of you know someone who's been an adult for 25? Don't have $73,000, right? That's all of us, right? You want to raise the bar again? Now I'm going to tell you something. If I keep raising the bar pretty soon you're going to run out of answers. I don't know how to get that. You want me to see? 6%. 6%. If you invested $165 a month for 25 years you would have about $114,000 in your bank account in 25 years. Is that getting pretty warm? That's pretty good, isn't it? Where can you get 6%? CD maybe, right? Well, where do you buy a CD? At a bank. Now I'm confused. You go into a bank, that's where you're going to get the 3% won't it? What do you do? You go into a bank and you say, hey, I want money and they say, well, we'll pay you 3% or we'll pay you 6%. Well, if you have that choice why would anybody choose 3%? Oh, liquidity, what does that mean? Oh you can get to it easier, right? See how easy it is to violate the wealth formula? What was the commitment you made about the wealth formula? An entire society said, no, I want to put it in a savings account because some people have not been taught the formula. The formulas take a certain amount of money and put it away and leave it alone. Under leave it alone, let it work, you would have to have the higher yield, wouldn't you? Okay, you'd have $114,000. You want to raise the bar? $165 a month? 10 months or 25 years? At 10% you would have $218,000 at the end of 25 years. Now every hand in this room should go up when I ask you how many of you know someone who's been an adult for 25 years don't have $218,000 every hand in here, right? But now wait a minute, where could you get 10% yield on your money? I want you to listen to the answers. You notice that fewer of you are speaking out now? Because as the bar gets higher, what was the bar about? What yield will you learn how to get? Evidently, we don't have a lot of people learning how to get a higher yield or we'd have a lot more answers here, wouldn't we? Did you know consistently over the last 30 or 40 years if you would find someone who knows how to manage money and they would just put together an investment fund and manage your money. You don't have to do anything, just put your $165 in their fund. They can make 20, 30, 40% interest, but what they'll do is consistent, they'll pay you around 10, it's called a mutual fund, a mutual fund. But you know most people do not know about mutual funds. It means you have to know to go to a brokerage house to set up your account, not the bank. It means you need to ask for something safe, not speculative. Okay, but good mutual funds can pay, over the last 30 or 40 years they've consistently paid about 10%. You want to raise the bar? Put 15% down there. 15%, $165 a month for 25 years at $15,000. 15% will give you $535,000. Are we getting close to where we want to be? If you knew how to invest your money at 15%, where can you invest money at 15%? Okay, let's go one more notch here. Let's go to 20%. $165 a month, 25 years at 20% would give you $1,400,000. Have we reached our objective here? So now are we setting some parameters here? How much yield do you need to learn how to get? Somewhere between 15% and 20%, would you agree with me on that? Okay now, I want to introduce to you a topic called tax lien certificates. Tax lien certificates are very simple. The government sets a budget. The budget is fulfilled by people paying their property taxes. And we're talking about counties. If people do not pay the property taxes, the county doesn't have the money to operate. They need that money. So they put penalties on those people who do not pay. It's called interest. And it's heavy. Let's assume we're at 15% to 20%, depending on the state and depending on the county. All states do not do this. All counties do not do this. Some have interest. Some have penalties. Or we're getting into some variables here which you might need to study this. But it doesn't change the fact that in February, we're going to have...one of my students is going to be doing a training on tax lien certificates. And I talked to her about this. At our speaker training, we teach people how to lower your price in the beginning to get people in there. And then raise your prices. You get better. Now, I have a good friend that charges $2,500 for this training on tax lien certificates. And what he does is he says, okay, you come and we'll teach you for three days, we'll teach you how all this works. And then we're going to go over to tax sale and we're going to buy them. So is that a good process? Hey, not only do I learn it, then I go over here and I start raising my...I want that one and that one and that one and that one. And you get your money, you buy the tax lien certificate for whatever the bid is and whatever you buy, you get 16%, 18% for that. Now one of my students says, well, I'll do that for $495. How many of you would like to go to the same type of training for 500 bucks? You learn how to do that, right? Okay, that's what this is all about. Where do you learn? You go somewhere to learn this. All right, now, let me tell you, in Arizona, and I'm going to teach you, you don't have to go to any seminar, go to Arizona in February and every county in Arizona will sell the tax certificates. Now are they safe? Ha, are they safe? When you buy the tax lien certificate, you get right in front of the mortgage holder in priority. You can foreclose for the amount of the taxes. You think the lien holder is going to allow you to do that? No, they'll come pay you. So the thing is this, you talk about safe, this is safe. You give the money to the government, you collect the money from the government. When that homeowner finally decides to pay their taxes, they pay the taxes that you paid the county, but the interest, you get that. And in Arizona, it's 16 percent. State of Mississippi, it's 18 percent. And Georgia and Texas, there's not interest, it's called a penalty. In Georgia, it's a 20 percent penalty. Means if you're one day late, you have to pay a 20 percent penalty. If they pay you the next day, you get 20 percent on your money and you already got your money back. And Texas, it's a 25 percent penalty. You didn't even know these things existed most of you, right? Okay, tax lien certificate, anywhere from 15, 20, sometimes 25 percent. You mean that one time a year, I could go take my money on an investment account, go to these tax lien sales and buy as many as I have money? Yes? Could you consistently get 15, 20 percent return on your money? You want to up the bar? You notice that it's all based, is that a big number there that keeps getting bigger? It's based on one thing and one thing only, what? What yield will you learn how to get? Let's put it up 25 percent. What yield is that on your money if you paid cash for the darn thing? 25 percent. And that's done all the time. You go pay cash for the house, fix it up, paint the front of it up, let me give you four things you can do to house to give you immediate value. Number one, mow the lawn. Number two, put fertilizer on the front lawn. Edge the front lawn. Paint the front of the house. Now if it's brick, just paint the trim on the front. Let's ask the experts out here, you ladies, why do you only put makeup on the front? The first impressions are everything, aren't they? I hardly ever use a PowerPoint presentation. I teach in a workshop format. I make people right. You will find that if you make people get involved, if you have something that's valuable, they'll write it down. If you put it up here, for some reason, they don't write it down. And I'll tell you this. If you didn't write this stuff down, you are not participating and your chance of being a successful presenter are very slim. Because I'm going to tell you something. If you want to learn, you learn by doing. You do not learn that much by just observing. So we're in the time out section. Let's look at that chunk and you tell me what it did for your presentation. Number one, is it a true formula? Absolutely. Number two, is it probably going to be wonderful information for everybody in your audience? Absolutely. And it affects their belief system. For the first time in their life, they can see something working in their life. But you keep going back to one thing. If you know someone that this is not working for them, one of two things either occurred. They never learned, that's called lack of knowledge, the wealth formula, or they did not implement, called lack of discipline. Is this tying into what Napoleon Hill talked earlier in your presentation? Now are we really working on teaching people the process of becoming wealthy? Absolutely. With this information, they can leave your seminar and actually go out and make money. But is it going to take them 25 years? Now it will take them 8 years, right? You think that they're now coming up with an idea of good gracious? If I would simply take some of my money now and learn more, I already see I can go faster. But I might make more money. I call it speed and greed. I want more money and I want it faster. Well in that case, maybe you ought to learn more. And you're showing people an exact amount of money that they can put to the value of coming to your training, depending on what you're going to have. Now if you're teaching philosophy, let's call it motivational principles, what were the principles that would have value to them there? Consistency, discipline. In other words, there are elements of success that you need to learn. And that's not something that you just write down and say, oh yeah, yeah I got that. Are these characteristics that you need to study, to learn, to build inside of you that become part of you? Okay, so you've got two choices here. You can either sell or train them and the skills have been successful, or you can train them and the skills of making more money. It's an option to you. They're going to need both. If you are teaching the methods of, if you're teaching the financial skills, the strategies of investing in real estate, the stock market, the things that you should, could you sell someone else's home study course on success building principles? Okay, now if you're teaching success building principles, could you sell somebody else's course on strategies of investing in the stock market or real estate? You see how this kind of thing works. People need it all. But your job is to show them why they need it all. They need to learn. Knowledge is the key. Y'all ready for another chunk? Was that one powerful, by the way?
